data.problem: A coffee shop is selling lattes and muffins. On the first day of sales, they sold 40 lattes and 20 muffins for a total of $640. They took in $700 for 40 lattes and 25 muffins on the second day. Find the price of each type of item.
data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } l &\text{ = price of latte} \\ 
\text{Let } m &\text{ = price of muffin} \\ 
 \\ 
40l + 20m &= 640 \\ 
40l + 25m &= 700 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
5m &= 60 \\ 
m &= 12 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
40l + 20(12) &= 640 \\ 
40l + 240 &= 640 \\ 
40l &= 400 \\ 
l &= 10
\end{aligned}

data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } s &\text{ = price of student ticket} \\ 
\text{Let } g &\text{ = price of general admission ticket} \\ 
 \\ 
30s + 10g &= 550 \\ 
30s + 20g &= 800 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
10g &= 250 \\ 
g &= 25 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
30s + 10(25) &= 550 \\ 
30s + 250 &= 550 \\ 
30s &= 300 \\ 
s &= 10
\end{aligned}
